# About the Author {.unnumbered}

Vijay Janapa Reddi is the Gordon McKay Professor of Electrical Engineering at
Harvard University. His work sits at the
boundary of computer architecture, runtime systems, edge computing, and
machine learning systems, with a recurring focus on how measurements,
benchmarks, and shared artifacts turn systems ideas into practice. As a
founding board member of MLCommons and an early co-chair of MLPerf Inference,
he has helped make machine-learning performance claims measurable and
reproducible. Architecture 2.0 grows from a conviction that runs through that
work. Good engineering is judged by the quality of what you build and by the
evidence supporting its efficiency, safety, robustness, and reliability, not
by the tools or methods you choose. This book applies that conviction to
AI-assisted computer architecture.

```{=latex}
\mainmatter
```
